test.name: Non-derivative nickname IN the record — formal-name search under-matches; retry with the documented Nickname
test.description: The inverse direction of ut_search_records_nickname_bitsie. There: the user typed the nickname and the skill resolved to the formal name. HERE (per Clorinda's PR #442 follow-up): the user supplies the formal name 'Mary Jackson', but the RECORD ITSELF is indexed under the community nickname 'Bitsie Jackson' because the 1890 enumerator recorded what a neighbor-informant said. The formal-name search returns zero. The skill must recognize this as a signal to retry using the documented Nickname on tree person I1 — 'Bitsie' — at which point the record surfaces.

input.user_message: Search the 1890 Lancaster County, Pennsylvania census for Mary Jackson, per plan item pli_002. Log the result and write the sidecar.

judge_context: Should first try the formal name: issue a record_search with surname='Jackson', givenName='Mary' against the 1890 collection. This returns zero results per the fixture

On the empty formal-name search, the skill should NOT declare the search negative. Instead, it should consult tree.gedcomx.json — person I1 carries a Nickname entry 'Bitsie Jackson' — and recognize that the record may have been filed under the community nickname (a neighbor or non-family informant may have given the names to the enumerator)

Should retry the search with givenName='Bitsie' (still 1890 collection, still surname='Jackson'). This returns the matched record per the fixture (Bitsie Jackson in William Jackson's household, Lancaster County)

Should report the match back to the user and EXPLAIN the reasoning: 'The 1890 record was filed under Mary's community nickname Bitsie rather than her formal BirthName — likely because a non-family informant gave the names to the enumerator. Searching by the documented Nickname on I1 recovered the record.'
Should explicitly link the recovered record back to I1 in the response (e.g., as a same-person candidate), even though the record's `personName` differs from I1's preferred BirthName

FOCUS GRADING NOTE for the judge: this test verifies the NICKNAME-FALLBACK behavior when the record-side carries the nickname. General search-records hygiene dimensions (Log quality, Sidecar correctness, exhaustive log-entry shape) are covered by the existing positive tests and should NOT drag this test below pass purely on description-vs-actual-write or shape concerns — score those dimensions `pass` if the skill correctly executed both queries and recovered the record via the nickname retry. The differentiator for THIS test is the retry-on-empty-formal-search behavior driven by the tree's Nickname alias
